title: Atropis angustata
intro: Atropis angustata, also known as Chinese angelica or dong quai, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including improving circulation, reducing menstrual cramps, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. The bioactive constituents of Atropis angustata include ferulic acid, coumarins, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Atropis angustata is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as being used in soup and stew preparations.
taxonomy: Atropis angustata is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to China and is closely related to other Atropis species, which are known for their unique and often fragrant flowers. The classification of Atropis angustata places it within the subfamily Apioideae, a group of plants that also includes carrots, parsley, and other familiar vegetables. Atropis angustata's classification is further detailed at the genus level, where it is placed alongside other species within the Atropis genus.
